title: Import groups from excel
excerpt:
Hello, 
is there a way to import from excel and having the groups created automatically? I have my project in excel and is already divided into groups (xls row) but when I do the import monday recognizes everything as ta…

posts.author: Massimaz (Massimiliano Mazzocchetti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

is there a way to import from excel and having the groups created automatically? I have my project in excel and is already divided into groups (xls row) but when I do the import monday recognizes everything as tasks and i need to create all groups manually.

thanks
Max

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this.
there has to be some kind of procedure to copy and paste and generate automatically the structure of group and pulses.

posts.author: blocker (Ben)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone ever figured this out yet?

posts.author: wallacebmann (Wallace Mann)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
I even tried to export an existing board with multiple groups into excel and then re-imported it. No go. It still lumps all the tasks into one group.

The fact that you can’t make a round trip export-to-import seems … less than ideal.

The problem is that the export to excel groups the data visually but does not adhere to any consistent use of columns. Ugh.

posts.author: cat.goliath (Cat)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

Would really love a solution to this! For me, I would like to choose the groups during the importing process. Perhaps the group is named in a column similar to how a column is selected to represent the item’s name.

I upload the same data over and over (with changing dates) and I just need them to go into the same group every time, instead of having to merge everything and delete extra groups!

posts.author: WimCos (Wim)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems such a basic feature that I really don’t understand why it isn’t available.

posts.author: RichardB (Richard)
posts.cookedHtml: Very disappointed to be adding to this - it was started 2yrs ago!! Surely this can be done. I reckon that importing ‘Groups’ from excel would be useful to close to 100% of users. How does one reach out to monday.com about this sort of thing?

posts.author: johngcolejr (John Cole, Jr)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would be very helpful. Still no solution I assume?

As a workaround, I’m using a trash column and then using that column to filter and move multiple items to groups.

Seems like a simple automation that could be added.

posts.author: Carleen (Carleen Dark Bays)
posts.cookedHtml: This should be a part of the import functionality. It asks about first row and column… it would be a logical next step to have a checkbox if you want all duplicates in first column to be grouped. Boom. Shouldn’t that be an easy fix for Monday to implement?

posts.author: JamesLacer (James)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 on this request from 4 years ago…

The largest disappointment about this, is that it limits the functionality of importing monday.com project plans from other accounts.

Given the assumed simplicity of this feature request, the obvious use cases, and the time this feature request has been present… It leads me to believe this is a intentional limitation of the system.

posts.author: iwoodford (Ian Woodford)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday exports boards with groups, but will not import with group or subitem functionality. Mondays’ inability to handle things in bulk is the number one limitation for large projects. Which is ironic, because small projects don’t need work management software.

posts.author: jamespatten (James Patten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Massimaz,

If you’re still looking for this, then you can import an Excel/CSV into an existing group with the monday app Data Importer

I’ve put together a tutorial specifically for this too.

It can’t currently create multiple groups off a single import which is what I think your use case is looking for, though this is something I could certainly look to build in. But thought I’d share the current functionality anyway in case it helps!

James

Screenshot 2025-01-04 at 11.52.091058×727 28.8 KB

posts.author: jaclynvogel (jaclyn vogel)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding another vote. I feel like this could be an easy import feature just by adding a column that maps to Groups. Or temporarily creating a label that corresponds to existing groups and automatically matches based on that, then erases the label column once the import is complete.

posts.author: jaclynvogel (jaclyn vogel)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates? I appreciate your recommendation, however it would require several imports for my current dataset so this would be quite inefficient (e.g. because some groups only have one item at this time). I’d love it if there was a way to map each item to a group upon import either by having a column that connects to the board groups or some other mechanism.

posts.author: jamespatten (James Patten)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jaclynvogel,

Good timing. I’ve now pushed an update so that you can import into multiple items with a single import. Here’s a tutorial:

dataimporter.co

How to import subitems into multiple monday items from Excel (Integration)

Import subitems from Excel into multiple monday subitems.

posts.author: hackercules (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @jaclynvogel !

Will Split to groups monday app suit your case? It allows to move selected items to the groups determined by column values. So if your import data contains group column, you can easily split imported items between groups specified in that column.

Of course native solution would be better, but at least something to simplify the work.
